data.narrative:
Neros is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Soren Monroe-Anderson and Olaf Hichwa that has raised $121M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparables: 8 private companies at the same stage and sector (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, and Castelion among them) plus 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $121M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a $230M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 8 same-sector, same-stage private comparables yields a $1.1B median, with the peer distribution ranging from $300M to $1.5B. The peer-median method carries more weight given the direct relevance of same-stage, same-sector positioning.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $195M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $677M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around whether Neros is positioned at the lower end of peer performance or tracking toward the upper quartile. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory relative to peers, customer concentration in government contracting, and technology differentiation within the crowded defense-tech Series B landscape. Additional disclosure of revenue and deployment metrics would narrow this band materially.
